samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="state0">Initial state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state1">Detecting edges.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state2">Rising edge detected.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state3">Falling edge detected.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state">State variable.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="next_state">Next state variable.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="rise_reg">Rising edge output register.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="down_reg">Falling edge output register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. It uses a state machine to monitor changes in the input signal. On the positive edge of the clock, it updates the current state and the rising and falling edge output registers based on the input signal. If a rising edge is detected, the rise output is set to 1, and if a falling edge is detected, the down output is set to 1. The module resets to the initial state on a low reset signal.
    </implementation>
</module>
